steps.title: Distribute the factor
steps.content:
Use the distributive property because the factor outside the parentheses multiplies every term inside. Multiply $5$ by the $x^2$ term and also multiply $5$ by the constant term:
$$5(x^2+7)=5\cdot x^2+5\cdot7$$

steps.title: Simplify the expression
steps.content: Evaluate the products and keep the terms combined only when they are like terms. The $x^2$ term stays separate from the constant: $$5\cdot x^2+5\cdot7=5x^2+35$$ The answer is A.
